---
title: "2025_AFDW_emergence"
author: "Chloé Gilligan"
date: "2026-03-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(readr)
library(lme4)
library(car) 
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse) 
library(dplyr)
library(lmerTest)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(dunn.test)
```

#Load data
```{r}
afdw <- read_csv("../data/2025_AFDW_sorted_filtered.csv")
meas <- read_csv("../data/NL_2025_master_measurements.csv")
```

#Merge
```{r}
afdw <- afdw %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_Code = str_extract(Full.Sample.ID, "^[^_]+"),
    Snail_Num   = as.integer(str_extract(Full.Sample.ID, "(?<=_)\\d+$"))
  )

meas_clean <- meas %>%
  select(Sample_Code = sample_name, Snail_Num = snail_number, length, width) %>%
  mutate(Snail_Num = as.integer(Snail_Num))

merged <- left_join(afdw, meas_clean, by = c("Sample_Code", "Snail_Num"))
```

#Decode merged labels into separate Temperature and Risk factors

```{r}

merged <- merged %>%
  mutate(
    Temperature = recode(Color,
      "B" = "Ambient",
      "G" = "Ambient",
      "R" = "Warming",
      "P" = "Warming"
    ),
    Risk = recode(Color,
      "B" = "Present",
      "G" = "Absent",
      "R" = "Present",
      "P" = "Absent"
    ),
    Treatment = recode(Color,
      "B" = "Ambient + Crab",
      "G" = "Ambient + No Crab",
      "R" = "Heated + Crab",
      "P" = "Heated + No Crab"
    ),
    Temperature = factor(Temperature, levels = c("Ambient", "Warming")),
    Risk        = factor(Risk,        levels = c("Absent", "Present"))
  )

```


#RIG4- outlier in the 4's when everything else is a 1
```{r}
merged <- merged %>% filter(Full.Sample.ID != "RIG4_1")
```

#Removing negative outliers
```{r}
# BRR1_2 and RIR1_2 - negative tissue mass
# RIG4_1 and BRP2_1 - negative shell mass
merged <- merged %>%
  filter(!Full.Sample.ID %in% c("BRR1_2", "RIR1_2", "RIG4_1", "BRP2_1"))
```


#Reorganizing columns
```{r}
merged <- merged %>%
  select(Tin, Full.Sample.ID, Sample_Code, Snail_Num,
         Treatment, Temperature, Risk, Color,
         Pre.burned.pan, Dry.Snail.Weight, Ashed.Snails.Ashed.Tin,
         Date.weighed, Date.ashed, Tissue.Mass, Shell.Mass, length, width)
```


#Exporting merged data
```{r}
write_csv(merged, "../data/2025_AFDW_merged.csv")
```


#Updated plots

```{r}
#Plotting
pal_temp <- c("Ambient" = "#1C86EE", "Warming" = "#EE0000")

# Tissue Mass
p_tissue <- ggplot(merged, aes(x = Risk, y = Tissue.Mass, color = Temperature)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4,
               position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8,
               position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_temp) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Absent" = "No Crab", "Present" = "Crab")) +
  labs(x = "Predation Risk", y = "Tissue Mass (g)", color = "Temperature") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# Shell Mass
p_shell <- ggplot(merged, aes(x = Risk, y = Shell.Mass, color = Temperature)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4,
               position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8,
               position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_temp) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Absent" = "No Crab", "Present" = "Crab")) +
  labs(x = "Predation Risk", y = "Shell Mass (g)", color = "Temperature") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggarrange(p_tissue, p_shell, ncol = 2, labels = c("A", "B"),
          common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right")
```


#stats

#Mean, SD, and n for each treatment group
```{r}
stats <- merged %>%
  group_by(Temperature, Risk) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    mean_tissue = mean(Tissue.Mass, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tissue   = sd(Tissue.Mass,   na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_shell  = mean(Shell.Mass,  na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_shell    = sd(Shell.Mass,    na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(stats)
```

#Stats
```{r}
# Tissue Mass
tissue_aov <- lm(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
Anova(tissue_aov, type = 3)

#Shell Mass
shell_aov <- lm(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
Anova(shell_aov, type = 3)

#TukeyHSD(aov(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
#TukeyHSD(aov(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
```

# Stats
```{r}
options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.poly"))

leveneTest(Tissue.Mass ~ Temperature * Risk, data = merged)
leveneTest(Shell.Mass  ~ Temperature * Risk, data = merged)

shapiro.test(merged$Tissue.Mass)
shapiro.test(merged$Shell.Mass)
```

```{r}
# Tissue Mass
m1_tissue <- lmer(Tissue.Mass ~ Temperature * Risk + (1|Sample_Code),
                  data = merged, REML = TRUE)
qqnorm(residuals(m1_tissue), main = "QQ Plot - Tissue Mass")
qqline(residuals(m1_tissue), col = "red", lwd = 2)
Anova(m1_tissue, test = "F", type = "III")
summary(m1_tissue)

# Shell Mass
m1_shell <- lmer(Shell.Mass ~ Temperature * Risk + (1|Sample_Code),
                 data = merged, REML = TRUE)
qqnorm(residuals(m1_shell), main = "QQ Plot - Shell Mass")
qqline(residuals(m1_shell), col = "red", lwd = 2)
Anova(m1_shell, test = "F", type = "III")
summary(m1_shell)
```


#Updated stats with Temperature and Risk as separate factors
```{r}
# Set contrasts
options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.poly"))

# Levene's test
leveneTest(Tissue.Mass ~ Temperature * Risk, data = merged)
leveneTest(Shell.Mass  ~ Temperature * Risk, data = merged)

# --- Tissue Mass ---
# Model 1: random effects for site and batch
m1_tissue <- lmer(Tissue.Mass ~ Temperature * Risk + (1|Sample_Code),
                  data = merged, REML = TRUE)
qqnorm(residuals(m1_tissue), main = "QQ Plot - Tissue Mass Model 1")
qqline(residuals(m1_tissue), col = "red", lwd = 2)
Anova(m1_tissue, test = "F", type = "III")
summary(m1_tissue)

# --- Shell Mass ---
m1_shell <- lmer(Shell.Mass ~ Temperature * Risk + (1|Sample_Code),
                 data = merged, REML = TRUE)
qqnorm(residuals(m1_shell), main = "QQ Plot - Shell Mass Model 1")
qqline(residuals(m1_shell), col = "red", lwd = 2)
Anova(m1_shell, test = "F", type = "III")
summary(m1_shell)
```
